publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-05-21 18:44:50 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Manuscript scores and notebooks containing Ann Silsbee's musical compositions.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ann Silsbee music compositions, #8639. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Ann Silsbee received her BA in Music from Radcliffe in 1951, her MS in Music from Syracuse University in 1969, and her Doctorate in composition from Cornell University in 1979. A pianist and composer, she wrote musical works in several genres, and is believed to be the first woman to have received a doctorate in composition from Cornell's Department of Music. She was also a poet and a painter, and the wife of Cornell physicist Robert Silsbee.

did.unittitle: Europe: Italy, Germany, Austria, Poland, Romania
N.Y. - Upstate New York - Long Island
Eastern United States, except New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: 7 Rituals, De Amore, Pharos II, Pathways, Runemusic

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes: Pathways, Glyphs, Letter from a Field Biologist, Quartet, Go Gentle, Corrai

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: includes: Pathways, Dona Nobis, Acre for a Bird, Pictures from Brueghel, Journey, D.B., Red Mountain Workshop

did.unittitle: Series V. Works by Anne Silsbee

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Notes have been made in both the scope and content and general notes about the type of composition and the parts.

did.unittitle: 3 Posthumous Concerts in Tribute to Ann Silsbee
